58: ## AR-034: Resource Quotas & Limits
59: 
60: ### Specification
61: 
62: **Owner:** Backend Team  
63: **Dependencies:** AR-007  
64: **Priority:** P1  
65: **Status:** 🔴 Not Started
66: 
67: ### Overview
68: Implement resource quota management to limit resource consumption per namespace, user, and session type. Prevent resource exhaustion and enable fair resource sharing.
69: 
70: ### Requirements
71: 
72: #### 1. Quota CRD Definition
73: ```yaml
74: apiVersion: runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh/v1alpha1
75: kind: ResourceQuota
76: metadata:
77:   name: team-a-quota
78:   namespace: production
79: spec:
80:   hard:
81:     # Session limits
82:     sessions.count: 100
83:     sessions.codeInterpreter: 50
84:     sessions.agentRuntime: 50
85:     
86:     # Compute limits
87:     cpu.request: "100"
88:     cpu.limit: "200"
89:     memory.request: "500Gi"
90:     memory.limit: "1000Gi"
91:     
92:     # Storage limits
93:     storage.ephemeral: "2Ti"
94:     storage.persistent: "10Ti"
95:     
96:     # Network limits
97:     network.egressPerSecond: "10Gbps"
98:     network.connectionsPerSession: 1000
99:     
100:     # Time limits
101:     session.maxDuration: 8h
102:     session.maxIdleTime: 30m
103: ```
104: 
105: #### 2. Quota Enforcement Points
106: 1. **Session Creation**: Reject if quota exceeded
107: 2. **Resource Updates**: Block if new total exceeds quota
108: 3. **Periodic Reconciliation**: Adjust for terminated sessions
109: 
110: #### 3. Quota Status
111: ```yaml
112: status:
113:   used:
114:     sessions.count: 45
115:     cpu.request: "45"
116:     memory.request: "225Gi"
117:   remaining:
118:     sessions.count: 55
119:     cpu.request: "55"
120:     memory.request: "275Gi"
121:   lastSyncTime: "2026-03-25T10:00:00Z"
122: ```
123: 
124: ### Implementation Tasks
125: 
126: - [ ] Define ResourceQuota CRD with kubebuilder markers
127: - [ ] Implement quota controller with informer cache
128: - [ ] Add quota validation to sandbox creation flow
129: - [ ] Implement resource usage tracking per namespace
130: - [ ] Add quota status updates and reconciliation
131: - [ ] Implement over-quota rejection with clear error messages
132: - [ ] Add quota metrics (usage, limits, violations)
133: - [ ] Write unit and integration tests
134: 
135: ### API Changes
136: 
137: **New Endpoints:**
138: ```
139: GET  /v1/namespaces/:namespace/resource-quotas
140: POST /v1/namespaces/:namespace/resource-quotas
141: GET  /v1/namespaces/:namespace/resource-quotas/:name
142: PUT  /v1/namespaces/:namespace/resource-quotas/:name
143: DELETE /v1/namespaces/:namespace/resource-quotas/:name
144: ```
145: 
146: ### Acceptance Criteria
147: - Quotas enforced at session creation time
148: - Quota status accurately reflects current usage
149: - Over-quota requests return 403 with detailed error
150: - Quota reconciliation runs every 60 seconds
151: - Metrics exposed for quota monitoring

155: ## AR-035: Secret Management
156: 
157: ### Specification
158: 
159: **Owner:** Backend Team  
160: **Dependencies:** AR-007, AR-037  
161: **Priority:** P0  
162: **Status:** 🔴 Not Started
163: 
164: ### Overview
165: Enable secure injection of secrets (API keys, credentials, certificates) into sandbox sessions without exposing them in pod specs or environment variables.
166: 
167: ### Requirements
168: 
169: #### 1. Secret CRD Definition
170: ```yaml
171: apiVersion: runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh/v1alpha1
172: kind: SessionSecret
173: metadata:
174:   name: openai-api-key
175:   namespace: production
176: spec:
177:   type: Opaque  # Opaque, TLS, BasicAuth
178:   data:
179:     # Base64 encoded values
180:     api_key: "base64encoded..."
181:     api_base: "base64encoded..."
182:   immutable: true
183:   
184:   # Access control
185:   allowedRuntimes:
186:     - name: my-agent
187:       namespace: production
188:   allowedUsers:
189:     - user@example.com
190: ```
191: 
192: #### 2. Secret Injection in AgentRuntime
193: ```yaml
194: apiVersion: runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh/v1alpha1
195: kind: AgentRuntime
196: metadata:
197:   name: my-agent
198: spec:
199:   secrets:
200:     - name: openai-api-key
201:       namespace: production
202:       envMappings:
203:         - secretKey: api_key
204:           envName: OPENAI_API_KEY
205:         - secretKey: api_base
206:           envName: OPENAI_API_BASE
207:       fileMappings:
208:         - secretKey: tls.crt
209:           mountPath: /etc/certs/tls.crt
210:           mode: 0400
211: ```
212: 
213: #### 3. Security Requirements
214: 1. **Encryption at Rest**: Secrets encrypted in etcd
215: 2. **Encryption in Transit**: TLS for all secret operations
216: 3. **Access Control**: RBAC for secret access
217: 4. **No Pod Spec Exposure**: Secrets injected via projected volumes
218: 5. **Audit Logging**: All secret access logged
219: 
220: ### Implementation Tasks

222: - [ ] Define SessionSecret CRD
223: - [ ] Implement secret controller with encryption
224: - [ ] Add secret injection to sandbox creation flow
225: - [ ] Implement projected volume mounting for secrets
226: - [ ] Add RBAC integration for secret access control
227: - [ ] Implement secret rotation mechanism
228: - [ ] Add audit logging for secret operations
229: - [ ] Write unit and integration tests
230: 
231: ### API Changes
232: 
233: **New Endpoints:**
234: ```
235: GET    /v1/namespaces/:namespace/secrets
236: POST   /v1/namespaces/:namespace/secrets

238: PUT    /v1/namespaces/:namespace/secrets/:name

251: ## AR-036: Session Persistence & Recovery
252: 
253: ### Specification
254: 
255: **Owner:** Backend Team  
256: **Dependencies:** AR-001, AR-007  
257: **Priority:** P1  
258: **Status:** 🔴 Not Started
259: 
260: ### Overview
261: Enable session state persistence and recovery for long-running agent sessions. Support checkpointing, migration, and disaster recovery scenarios.
262: 
263: ### Requirements
264: 
265: #### 1. Session State Types
266: ```go
267: type SessionState struct {
268:     SessionID    string                 `json:"sessionId"`
269:     CheckpointID string                 `json:"checkpointId"`
270:     Timestamp    time.Time              `json:"timestamp"`
271:     State        map[string]interface{} `json:"state"`
272:     Files        []FileState            `json:"files"`
273:     Memory       MemoryState            `json:"memory"`
274: }
275: 
276: type FileState struct {
277:     Path    string `json:"path"`
278:     Hash    string `json:"hash"`
279:     Size    int64  `json:"size"`
280:     Content []byte `json:"content,omitempty"`
281: }
282: 
283: type MemoryState struct {
284:     Variables map[string]string `json:"variables"`
285:     Context   map[string]interface{} `json:"context"`
286: }
287: ```
288: 
289: #### 2. Checkpointing Configuration
290: ```yaml
291: apiVersion: runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh/v1alpha1
292: kind: AgentRuntime
293: metadata:
294:   name: persistent-agent
295: spec:
296:   persistence:
297:     enabled: true
298:     checkpointInterval: 5m
299:     maxCheckpoints: 10
300:     storage:
301:       type: s3  # s3, gcs, azure, pvc
302:       s3:
303:         bucket: agentcube-checkpoints
304:         region: us-west-2
305:         prefix: sessions/
306:     stateToPersist:
307:       - memory
308:       - filesystem
309:       - environment
310: ```
311: 
312: #### 3. Recovery Scenarios
313: 1. **Pod Failure**: Auto-recover to last checkpoint
314: 2. **Node Migration**: Migrate session to new node
315: 3. **Manual Restore**: Restore from specific checkpoint
316: 4. **Scheduled Backup**: Periodic full state backup
317: 
318: ### Implementation Tasks
319: 
320: - [ ] Define session state data models
321: - [ ] Implement checkpoint controller
322: - [ ] Add state serialization for memory and filesystem
323: - [ ] Implement checkpoint storage backends (S3, GCS, PVC)
324: - [ ] Add session recovery logic on pod failure
325: - [ ] Implement checkpoint listing and restoration API
326: - [ ] Add checkpoint retention management
327: - [ ] Write unit and integration tests
328: 
329: ### API Changes
330: 
331: **New Endpoints:**
332: ```
333: POST   /v1/sessions/:sessionId/checkpoints
334: GET    /v1/sessions/:sessionId/checkpoints
335: GET    /v1/sessions/:sessionId/checkpoints/:checkpointId
336: POST   /v1/sessions/:sessionId/restore
337: POST   /v1/sessions/:sessionId/restore/:checkpointId
338: ```
339: 
340: ### Acceptance Criteria
341: - Sessions checkpoint every configured interval
342: - Checkpoints stored in configured backend
343: - Session auto-recovers on pod failure
344: - Manual restore from any checkpoint works
345: - Old checkpoints cleaned up based on retention policy
346:

349: ## AR-037: Multi-Tenant Isolation
350: 
351: ### Specification
352: 
353: **Owner:** Backend Team  
354: **Dependencies:** AR-007, AR-034  
355: **Priority:** P0  
356: **Status:** 🔴 Not Started
357: 
358: ### Overview
359: Implement strong multi-tenant isolation to ensure complete separation between different tenants' workloads, data, and resources.
360: 
361: ### Requirements
362: 
363: #### 1. Tenant CRD Definition
364: ```yaml
365: apiVersion: runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh/v1alpha1
366: kind: Tenant
367: metadata:
368:   name: acme-corp
369: spec:
370:   displayName: "Acme Corporation"
371:   admins:
372:     - admin@acme.com
373:   namespaces:
374:     - acme-production
375:     - acme-staging
376:   isolation:
377:     network:
378:       enabled: true
379:       allowEgress: false
380:       allowedEndpoints:
381:         - host: api.openai.com
382:           port: 443
383:     storage:
384:       enabled: true
385:       sharedVolumes: false
386:     compute:
387:       nodeSelector:
388:         tenant: acme-corp
389:       tolerations:
390:         - key: "dedicated"
391:           value: "acme"
392: ```
393: 
394: #### 2. Tenant Isolation Levels
395: 1. **Namespace Isolation**: Tenant workloads in dedicated namespaces
396: 2. **Network Isolation**: Network policies per tenant
397: 3. **Storage Isolation**: Separate storage classes or encryption keys
398: 4. **Compute Isolation**: Dedicated nodes or resource pools
399: 5. **Identity Isolation**: Separate identity providers per tenant
400: 
401: #### 3. Cross-Tenant Access
402: ```yaml
403: # Allow controlled cross-tenant access
404: apiVersion: runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh/v1alpha1
405: kind: TenantAccessPolicy
406: metadata:
407:   name: acme-to-partner
408: spec:
409:   sourceTenant: acme-corp
410:   targetTenant: partner-corp
411:   allowedOperations:
412:     - type: invoke
413:       resources:
414:         - namespace: partner-corp
415:           name: shared-api
416: ```
417: 
418: ### Implementation Tasks
419: 
420: - [ ] Define Tenant and TenantAccessPolicy CRDs
421: - [ ] Implement tenant controller
422: - [ ] Add namespace-scoping for all resources
423: - [ ] Implement network policy generation per tenant
424: - [ ] Add storage class isolation
425: - [ ] Implement node selector/taint enforcement
426: - [ ] Add tenant-level metrics and logging
427: - [ ] Write unit and integration tests
428: 
429: ### API Changes
430: 
431: **New Endpoints:**
432: ```
433: GET    /v1/tenants
434: POST   /v1/tenants
435: GET    /v1/tenants/:name
436: PUT    /v1/tenants/:name
437: DELETE /v1/tenants/:name
438: GET    /v1/tenants/:name/namespaces
439: GET    /v1/tenants/:name/quotas
440: ```
441: 
442: ### Acceptance Criteria
443: - Tenants cannot access other tenants' sessions
444: - Network policies isolate tenant traffic
445: - Storage is isolated per tenant
446: - Compute resources can be isolated to tenant nodes
447: - Cross-tenant access only via explicit policies

611:           averageUtilization: 70

613:       resource:
614:         name: memory
615:         target:

617:           averageUtilization: 80

696: ## AR-040: Policy Engine
697: 
698: ### Specification
699: 
700: **Owner:** Backend Team  
701: **Dependencies:** AR-007, AR-035  
702: **Priority:** P1  
703: **Status:** 🔴 Not Started
704: 
705: ### Overview
706: Implement a flexible policy engine for enforcing security, compliance, and operational rules across all AgentCube resources.
707: 
708: ### Requirements
709: 
710: #### 1. Policy CRD Definition
711: ```yaml
712: apiVersion: runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh/v1alpha1
713: kind: Policy
714: metadata:
715:   name: security-policy
716:   namespace: production
717: spec:
718:   enforcement: enforce  # enforce, audit, warn
719:   
720:   rules:
721:     # Network policy
722:     - name: restrict-egress
723:       target: Session
724:       condition: |
725:         session.spec.network.egress.enabled == true
726:       action: deny
727:       message: "Egress must be disabled in production"
728:     
729:     # Resource policy
730:     - name: max-session-duration
731:       target: AgentRuntime
732:       condition: |
733:         runtime.spec.sessionTimeout > duration("8h")
734:       action: deny
735:       message: "Session timeout cannot exceed 8 hours"
736:     
737:     # Image policy
738:     - name: approved-images-only
739:       target: AgentRuntime
740:       condition: |
741:         !runtime.spec.podTemplate.spec.containers[0].image.startsWith("approved-registry/")
742:       action: deny
743:       message: "Only approved registry images allowed"
744:     
745:     # Secret policy
746:     - name: required-secrets
747:       target: AgentRuntime
748:       condition: |
749:         runtime.spec.secrets.length == 0
750:       action: warn
751:       message: "No secrets configured for runtime"
752: ```
753: 
754: #### 2. Policy Types
755: 1. **Security Policies**: Network, image, privilege restrictions
756: 2. **Resource Policies**: Quota, limits, scheduling rules
757: 3. **Compliance Policies**: Audit, logging, retention requirements
758: 4. **Operational Policies**: Naming conventions, labeling requirements
759: 
760: #### 3. Policy Evaluation
761: ```go
762: type PolicyEngine interface {
763:     Evaluate(ctx context.Context, resource interface{}, action Action) (*EvaluationResult, error)
764: }
765: 
766: type EvaluationResult struct {
767:     Allowed   bool     `json:"allowed"`
768:     Violations []Violation `json:"violations"`
769:     Warnings   []string    `json:"warnings"`
770: }
771: ```
